name: Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop
name_original: Punta del Esparto y las Salinas — circular desde Cabo de Gata — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar
description: Mountain bike the 35.3-mile Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop, a difficult route through volcanic landscapes and pristine beaches.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alméria offers diverse landscapes, from Europe's only desert to volcanic coastlines and high mountain ranges. The region features arid, moon-like terrain with canyons and dry riverbeds in the Tabernas Desert, alongside the unique volcanic formations and coastal paths of Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. Inland, mountain ranges like Sierra Nevada and Sierra Alhamilla provide demanding passes and challenging climbs. This varied geography creates a wide array of terrain for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Alméria The most popular mountain bike trail is Presa de la Peseta — circular desde Almería, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop, a difficult 35.3 miles (56.8 km) path. This trail winds through the unique volcanic landscape and coastal features of Cabo de Gata. Local mountain bikers also love the Playa de las Amoladeras — circular desde Retamar — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail leading through coastal and volcanic terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Alméria is defined by desert landscapes, volcanic coastlines, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to highly technical descents. The routes in Alméria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alméria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Almería?
A: Almería offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy rides to more challenging and technical descents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What makes mountain biking in Almería unique?
A: Almería stands out due to its incredibly diverse landscapes, offering everything from Europe's only desert, the Tabernas Desert, to volcanic coastlines in Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, and high mountain ranges like the Sierra Nevada. This variety provides a unique backdrop for mountain biking, with trails winding through arid canyons, along pristine beaches, and up demanding mountain passes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Almería?
A: You can expect a wide array of terrain. The Tabernas Desert features stony paths, sandy sections, canyons, and dry riverbeds. In Cabo de Gata, trails wind through volcanic landscapes and along coastal paths. Mountainous areas like Sierra Alhamilla offer forest tracks and technical singletracks, ensuring a varied experience for all riders.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Almería?
A: Yes, Almería offers a good selection of trails suitable for beginners. Approximately 25 of the available routes are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: Where can I find more challenging mountain bike routes in Almería?
A: For more challenging rides, you'll find around 25 difficult routes in the Almería region. Areas like the Sierra Nevada foothills and parts of the Tabernas Desert offer demanding passes and significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013389696/transandalus-cabo-de-gata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable difficult route, winding through volcanic terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Almería?
A: Almería benefits from an exceptional climate with approximately 320 days of sunshine per year, making it an ideal year-round destination. However, it's advisable to avoid the central hours during intense summer heat. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for riding.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Almería?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Almería are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013553019/presa-de-la-peseta-circular-desde-almeria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de la Peseta — circular desde Almería&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 18.2-mile route with varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031289841/playa-de-las-amoladeras-circular-desde-retamar-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de las Amoladeras — circular desde Retamar — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 15.9-mile ride through coastal and volcanic landscapes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along mountain bike trails in Almería?
A: Many trails pass by significant landmarks. In the Tabernas Desert, you might encounter film locations from 'Spaghetti Westerns' or dramatic canyons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo&lt;/a&gt;. Along the coast, the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park offers unique volcanic formations and pristine beaches. You can also find historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2846137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Almería&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The diverse landscapes of Almería provide numerous scenic viewpoints. Coastal routes in Cabo de Gata offer stunning Mediterranean sea views, while mountain trails in areas like Sierra Alhamilla provide panoramic vistas. The Tabernas Desert also has unique viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lawrence of Arabia Oasis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Almería?
A: The mountain bike routes in Almería are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the region's incredible landscape diversity, from the unique desert scenery to the beautiful volcanic coastlines, and the variety of trails suitable for all skill levels.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails in the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park is a prime area for mountain biking. It features a unique volcanic landscape with trails winding through ancient geological formations, along dramatic cliffs, and beside clear Mediterranean waters. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013389696/transandalus-cabo-de-gata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031289841/playa-de-las-amoladeras-circular-desde-retamar-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de las Amoladeras — circular desde Retamar — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar&lt;/a&gt; are excellent options to explore this protected coastal area.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, some mountain biking routes in Almería offer the chance to pass through traditional white villages, especially in regions like the Alpujarras on the southern slopes of the Sierra Nevada. These routes often follow ancient mule trails, providing a glimpse into local culture and gastronomy.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is incredibly varied, offering everything from seafront paths and dedicated cycle lanes to dirt tracks and secondary roads. You'll encounter sections with sandy beaches where you might need to push your bike, as well as broken trails with loose sand and stones. The landscape features arid, volcanic environments with lava domes, fossil beaches, and areas of agaves and cacti, alongside stunning virgin beaches and crystal-clear coves.

faqs.question: How challenging is this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' overall, with a 'moderate' physical difficulty. It's best suited for riders with at least an intermediate level of fitness. Some sections can be quite demanding, potentially requiring you to push your bike through sand, streams, or over stones. We recommend a mountain bike with wide tires and good grip, ideally a full-suspension model, for comfort and to handle the diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this loop are during spring and autumn. Summer temperatures in Cabo de Gata can be extremely hot, making the ride much more challenging and less enjoyable. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant weather conditions for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route passes through the unique volcanic landscapes of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. You'll encounter dramatic cliffs, reefs, and pristine beaches like Genoveses or Mónsul. Highlights include the historic San Felipe Castle, the scenic Genoveses Coastal Trail, and various other cross-country and cliffside trails. You'll also pass through charming coastal villages and old gold mining areas like Rodalquilar.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there is generally no entrance fee to access the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park itself. However, as a protected natural area, it's important to respect all regulations, such as staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife, and practicing Leave No Trace principles. Specific activities or access to certain areas might have their own rules, so always check local signage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop?
faqs.answer: The Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop is designed to be ridden in stages, often starting and ending in towns with facilities. While there isn't one single designated 'trailhead parking' for the entire loop, you can typically find parking in the coastal villages and towns within or near the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, such as Cabo de Gata, San José, or Las Negras, where you can begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas and near wildlife. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and potential heat, as some sections might be difficult for dogs, particularly those involving sand or loose stones. Always carry enough water for your dog.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Transandalus: Cabo de Gata Loop?
faqs.answer: The full loop is approximately 56.8 kilometers (35.3 miles) long with about 657 meters (2,155 feet) of elevation gain. For an experienced mountain biker, it can take around 4.5 to 5 hours of riding time. However, given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's wise to plan for a full day, especially if you want to stop and enjoy the scenery or tackle it in stages.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is a segment of the much larger 2000-kilometer Transandalus route, which circles all eight provinces of Andalusia. The route also passes through sections of the Senda Azul de Cabo de Gata and the Loma Pelada, among other local paths.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain biking trip on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging terrain and semi-arid climate, you should pack plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and basic bike repair tools (spare tube, pump). A first-aid kit is also advisable. Due to the varied surfaces, wearing appropriate cycling gear, including padded shorts and gloves, will enhance your comfort.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food and accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is often broken into stages designed to end in towns and villages that offer accommodations, restaurants, and cafes. Coastal villages like San José, Las Negras, and Rodalquilar provide opportunities to refuel and rest, making it suitable for multi-day self-guided tours.
